trivia: The final evolution of the A300, the aircraft that started Airbus itself. The A300 was the world's first twin-engine widebody, and the -600 refined it with a two-crew cockpit and more range.
Passenger examples are nearly extinct; the surviving type identity mostly echoes through the freighter world it spawned.
funFacts: Descendant of the very first Airbus, the 1972 A300.
Its fuselage cross-section lives on in the A330 and A340.
